dropImage: Drop an image here or click to select
supportedFormats: Supports PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP, SVG
firstFrameOnly: Animated image — only the first frame is used
largeImage: Large image ({w}×{h}) — processing may be slow
formatNotSupported: This image format is not supported. Please use PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, or SVG.

descriptions.title: About Image Rotate
descriptions.aeoDefinition: Image Rotate is a free online tool for rotating and flipping images. Supports 90°, 180°, 270° rotation and horizontal/vertical flip. All processing runs locally in your browser.
descriptions.whatIs: Rotate images by 90°, 180°, or 270° and flip them horizontally or vertically. Combine rotation and flip for precise image orientation. No data is uploaded — all processing uses the HTML5 Canvas API.
descriptions.step1Text: Drag and drop an image onto the drop zone, or click to browse. Supports PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, and SVG input.
descriptions.step2Text: Select a rotation angle (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°) and optionally toggle horizontal or vertical flip.
descriptions.step3Text: Preview the rotated image and download it. The output format matches the input format.
descriptions.p1: Rotate and flip images by 90°, 180°, or 270° directly in your browser. Compress rotated images with [Image Compressor](/image-compress), or convert formats with [Image Converter](/image-convert).
descriptions.p2: Supports PNG, JPG, and WebP output. Images in GIF, BMP, SVG, or AVIF format are automatically converted to PNG.
descriptions.faq1Q: What image formats can I rotate?
descriptions.faq1A: You can rotate PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, BMP, SVG, and AVIF images. The output format matches the input format. Unsupported output formats (GIF, BMP, SVG, AVIF) are saved as PNG.
descriptions.faq2Q: Are my images uploaded to a server?
descriptions.faq2A: No. All image processing happens in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images never leave your device.
descriptions.faq3Q: Can I combine rotation and flip?
descriptions.faq3A: Yes. You can apply both rotation (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°) and flip (horizontal, vertical) at the same time. The flip is applied first, then the rotation.
